county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Liberty. Created 1821 from Wayne, Franklin, and Fayette counties. Union County is one of Indiana's smallest counties. Strong Quaker heritage — extensive Friends meeting records. Union County Historical Society. Whitewater River valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Liberty is a small county seat with well-preserved records. West College Corner straddles the Indiana-Ohio border — cross-reference Preble County, OH. Brownsville and Dunlapsville area records. Check Wayne, Franklin, and Fayette for pre-1821 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1821. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1821. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1821. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Union County Auditor, Liberty / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1821. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Union County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Union County
source_profiles.repository: Earlham College Friends Collection, Richmond / Indiana Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Indiana Yearly Meeting and Western Yearly Meeting records. Quakers kept meticulous records. Indiana was a major destination for Quaker migration from the Carolinas and Virginia — check origin meeting records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed by FamilySearch and held at Earlham College

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Evansville. Created 1818 from Gibson, Posey, and Warrick counties. Evansville is southwestern Indiana's largest city and a major Ohio River port. Willard Library (1885) in Evansville has outstanding genealogy collection. Vanderburgh County Historical Society. Evansville Museum archives. Major industrial center — Whirlpool, Mead Johnson, and other factory records. Strong German immigrant community (Germantown area). African American community records important. Cross-reference Henderson County, KY across the river. Evansville city directories from 1858.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1818. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1818. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1818. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Vanderburgh County Auditor, Evansville / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1818. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Vanderburgh County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Vanderburgh County
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Indianapolis / Diocese of Evansville / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. St. Meinrad Archabbey (Dubois/Spencer) holds significant church records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio River Commerce Records — Vanderburgh County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Local historical societies / Kentucky river communities
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Steamboat records, ferry licenses, river pilot records, and flatboat commerce. Ohio River counties had close economic ties to Kentucky — cross-reference Kentucky county records across the river. Early Indiana settlement came primarily via the Ohio River.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ferry licenses at county level; steamboat records at NARA and historical societies

source_profiles.repository: Vanderburgh County Clerk, Evansville / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Newport. Created 1824 from Parke County. Wabash River county on the Illinois border. Vermillion County Historical Society. Clinton was a major coal mining town — mine employment records document immigrant workers. Cayuga and Dana area records. Strong labor movement records — Eugene V. Debs (Terre Haute) connections. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Ernie Pyle birthplace (Dana). Check Parke County for pre-1824 records. Coal mining brought Italian and Eastern European immigrants.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1824. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1824. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1824. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Vermillion County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Coal Mining Employment Records — Vermillion County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Coal Council / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana coal mine employment records, company town records, and United Mine Workers (UMW) union records. Coal mining attracted Italian, Eastern European, and African American workers. Mine inspection reports at Indiana State Archives list workers. Company store ledgers document families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine records scattered; Indiana Bureau of Mines reports at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Terre Haute. Created 1818 from Sullivan County. Terre Haute was a major industrial and transportation center. Vigo County Historical Society. Indiana State University (1865) archives. Eugene V. Debs home and museum — labor movement records. Wabash River valley. Terre Haute had diverse immigrant communities: German, Irish, Italian. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Clabber Girl baking powder and other industrial records. Coal mining in eastern portion. Prairie Creek and Honey Creek settlements. Vigo County Public Library has genealogy collection.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1818. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1818. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1818. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Vigo County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Vigo County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

source_profiles.source_name: Coal Mining Employment Records — Vigo County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Coal Council / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana coal mine employment records, company town records, and United Mine Workers (UMW) union records. Coal mining attracted Italian, Eastern European, and African American workers. Mine inspection reports at Indiana State Archives list workers. Company store ledgers document families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wabash. Created 1832 from Cass and Grant counties. Wabash was the first electrically lighted city in the world (1880). Wabash County Historical Museum. Significant Miami Indian presence — Treaty of Paradise Spring (1826) and Miami allotments. Some Miami families received land allotments and remained in Wabash County. Wabash and Erie Canal records. BLM-GLO federal land patents. North Manchester (Manchester University, Church of the Brethren) area records. Lagro and Roann area records. Check Cass and Grant for pre-1832 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1832. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1832. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Wabash County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Washington DC / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Miami Nation records including treaties, removal records (1846), land allotments, annuity rolls, and agent correspondence. Some Miami families received individual land allotments and remained in Indiana. Chief Richardville (Peshewah) and Meshingomesia's band records. Treaty of St. Mary's (1818), Treaty of Paradise Spring (1826).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records at NARA Record Group 75; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. Canal workers included many Irish immigrants. Canal land grants at Indiana State Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Indiana State Archives; some published by Indiana Historical Bureau

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Williamsport. Created 1827 from Fountain County. Warren County Historical Society. Wabash River and Pine Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Williamsport Falls area. West Lebanon and Rainsville area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Agricultural county with well-preserved records. Check Fountain County for pre-1827 records. One of Indiana's less populous counties — township records important supplements.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1827. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Boonville. Created 1813 from Knox County. Warrick County Historical Society and Museum in Boonville. Ohio River access via Newburgh — steamboat records. Abraham Lincoln worked on the Jones store and farm in Warrick County as a youth. Coal mining heritage — mine records in Lynnville and Boonville area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records from early settlement. Newburgh and Chandler area records. Check Knox County for pre-1813 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1813. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Salem. Created 1813 from Clark, Harrison, and Jefferson counties. Salem was an important early Indiana town — Washington County Historical Society. John Hay birthplace (Lincoln's private secretary). Underground Railroad activity documented — Levi Coffin connections. Blue River and East Fork White River valleys. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Pekin and Campbellsburg area records. Beck's Mill historical site. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Check parent counties for pre-1813 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1813. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Clark's Grant Land Records — Washington County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives, Indianapolis / Indiana State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: George Rogers Clark's Illinois Regiment land grant (150,000 acres) in southeastern Indiana. Soldiers who served under Clark in the Revolutionary War received land warrants. This area was NOT part of the federal land system — use Virginia/Illinois records, not BLM-GLO. Early Clarksville (1784) was Indiana's first American settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Indiana State Archives and Indiana State Library; some published in Indiana land record compilations

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Washington County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Richmond. Created 1810 from Clark and Dearborn counties. Richmond is a major Quaker center — Earlham College (1847) and its Friends Collection and Archives hold outstanding Quaker genealogical records. Wayne County Historical Museum. Richmond was an important stop on the Underground Railroad — Levi Coffin House is in nearby Fountain City (north Wayne County). Whitewater River valley. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong industrial records (Richmond Casket Co., Crosley). Indiana German heritage in Hagerstown area. Centerville, Cambridge City, and Fountain City records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.repository: Wayne County Clerk, Richmond / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bluffton. Created 1835 from Allen, Randolph, and Delaware counties. Wells County Historical Society. Wabash River headwaters area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Bluffton and Ossian area records. Swiss Mennonite and Amish heritage. Strong agricultural community — township records important. Petroleum and Uniondale area records. Indiana natural gas boom brought growth in 1890s. Check parent counties for pre-1835 records. Well-preserved county records from 1837.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1835. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Wells County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Wells County
source_profiles.repository: Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen College / Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget and Die Botschaft newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Records may be in German/Pennsylvania Dutch. Mennonite Historical Library at Goshen College is a premier repository. Family histories compiled by Amish genealogists are often in private collections.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records in private family collections; Mennonite Historical Library has compiled indexes and microfilm
